patterns.what_worked: Ecosystem detection (npm) was correct, build and test validation passed with 203/203 tests, npm audit reached 0 vulnerabilities
patterns.what_was_hard: Agent used broad npm audit fix approach instead of targeting the specific vulnerable package (minimatch). This caused 4+ unrelated packages to be updated including a major version bump of mocha (v10 to v11) which risks breaking changes. Developer only wanted minimatch fixed.
patterns.improvisation: Agent applied blanket npm audit remediation rather than surgically fixing the reported vulnerability. This over-remediated beyond the developers intent.
patterns.recurring_pattern: Dependency update agents tend to fix all audit findings rather than scoping to the specific reported vulnerability. Major version bumps without developer consent is a recurring friction point.
patterns.skill_gap: The Dependabot agent needs a mode to target a specific package/vulnerability rather than fixing all audit findings. Should also flag major version bumps for explicit developer approval before applying.
patterns.environment_blockers: GitHub EMU restrictions prevented PR creation on microsoft/tfs-cli

outcome.changes_proposed: Updated 4 direct dependencies (tmp, uuid, lodash, mocha) and added 8 npm overrides for transitive dependencies. Developer only wanted minimatch vulnerability fixed.
outcome.full_journey: Developer asked for ES5.2 help in [repo]. Orchestrator matched ES5.2 to Dependabot agent via fast path. Branch sfi/ES5.2/d9490d46 created. Agent scanned [repo], detected npm ecosystem, ran npm audit, and applied broad fix covering 12 vulnerabilities across 4 direct deps and 8 overrides. Build and 203 tests passed. However developer feedback indicates the target was specifically minimatch, and the agent over-remediated by bumping unrelated packages including a major version change (mocha v10 to v11). PR creation was blocked by GitHub EMU restrictions. Developer rated session 1/5 due to scope creep beyond intended fix.
